channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Insurgents reach gates of Syria’s capital, threatening to upend decades of Assad rule
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Insurgents' stunning march across Syria accelerated Saturday with news that they had reached the gates of the capital and that government forces had abandoned the central city of Homs. The government was forced to deny rumors that President Bashar Assad had fled the country.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire five years ago nearly destroyed the beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Storm Darragh batters UK and Ireland, leaving 1 dead and hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A man died and hundreds of thousands of people in Britain and Ireland were left without power Saturday as high winds and heavy rain battered the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about martial law and the impeachment vote threatening South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — For now, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has survived an impeachment attempt for sending heavily armed soldiers into Seoul’s streets with a baffling declaration of martial law that reminded many of the country’s past military-backed dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Effort to impeach South Korean president fails after ruling party walkout
item.description: Opposition leaders hoping to oust South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol over his brief declaration of martial law hit a roadblock Saturday, after members of the ruling party left the room, preventing parliament from reaching the threshold to impeach the president.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Following President Biden on his 'global finale' to Africa
item.description: Fox News' senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot reported from President Biden's trip to Angola, where the president announced major funding for projects in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 3 and injuring others
item.description: An explosion and fire has rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague, killing three people and injuring other people and destroying several apartments.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Syria's forces withdraw from Homs, a key link between the capital and coast
item.description: Syrian opposition fighters have reached the suburbs of the capital, Damascus, and government forces are withdrawing from the strategic city of Homs as the rebels’ surprising offensive picks up speed and President Bashar Assad’s whereabouts are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean president apologizes after attempting to impose martial law
item.description: South Korea’s President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized on Saturday for declaring martial law in the country for the first time since 1980, which sparked outrage from citizens and lawmakers alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: South Korea’s embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol has avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung And Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alternative healer gets 10 years in UK prison for death of woman at slap therapy workshop
item.description: Hongchi Xiao, a 61-year-old alternative healer who used 'slapping therapy' to treat maladies, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for the death of a 71-year-old woman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame's reopening will begin with an archbishop's knock on the doors. Here's what comes next
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening this weekend of Notre Dame is a succession of ceremonies to breathe life back into the iconic cathedral and celebrate the recovery from its devastating fire in 2019. Forecasts of stormy winds forced a late change in plans Friday, moving all of Saturday's events indoors.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-British soldier pleads not guilty to 'Bloody Sunday' murders after judge clears the way for trial
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British soldier will be tried for the murder of two men killed during the 1972 “Bloody Sunday” disturbances in Londonderry, Northern Ireland’s second-biggest city, after a judge refused to dismiss the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: U.S. raises alarm over Russian interference in Romania’s election
item.description: The Biden administration and lawmakers in Congress are raising alarm over Russian interference in presidential elections in Romania, a NATO ally that borders Ukraine and the Black Sea.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 12-year-old girl finds ancient Egyptian amulet in Israel
item.description: An ordinary family trip turned extraordinary when a 12-year-old girl found what turned out to be an ancient Egyptian amulet dating back 3,500 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kosovo accuses Serbia of 'terrorist attack' resembling Russian actions in Ukraine
item.description: Kosovo accused Serbia of carrying out a terrorist attack on a water pipeline in the Serb majority north of Kosovo. Serbia denies the accusations, but the recent bloodshed highlights that peace in the Balkans is far from certain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
